stem: A train leaves Station A traveling east at 60 miles per hour. Two hours later, a second train leaves the same station traveling east on the same route at 90 miles per hour. How many hours after the second train departs will it catch up to the first train?
explanation: Let t be the time in hours after the second train departs. When the second train catches up, both trains have traveled the same distance from Station A. The first train has been traveling for (t + 2) hours at 60 mph, covering 60(t + 2) miles. The second train has been traveling for t hours at 90 mph, covering 90t miles. Setting these equal: 60(t + 2) = 90t. Expanding: 60t + 120 = 90t. Subtracting 60t: 120 = 30t. Dividing by 30: t = 4 hours. Verification: First train travels 60(6) = 360 miles in 6 hours total. Second train travels 90(4) = 360 miles in 4 hours.

distractorRationale.A: Used the 2-hour head start as the answer without calculating catch-up time = 2.
distractorRationale.B: Found when the faster train covers the initial distance gap. 60(2) = 120 miles, then 120 ÷ (90 - 60) = 120 ÷ 30 = 4, but made error = 3.
distractorRationale.C: Correct answer: set 60(t + 2) = 90t, solved 120 = 30t to get t = 4 hours after the second train departs.
distractorRationale.D: Calculated total time for the first train instead of time after second train departs. t + 2 = 6 from correct calculation.
